parsed_review.summary: CP00-267 is a Risk C, 150-unit descriptor-only pack that closes the RP07.P08 Hermes tail (M06 Synthetic Fixture Set tail, M07-M10) and opens the RP07.P09 Claude review foundation (M00-M03 head). The pack-level production_ready claim is honestly FALSE (manifest status implemented_pending_review; command-evidence gate_outcome implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending), and the descriptor-only closure claim is fully supported. Baseline/prompt distributions reconcile exactly against the live closeout-pack-plan.json entry (l.412: risk C, 150 units, range RP07.P08.M06.S09-RP07.P09.M03.S10, ledger sha 13c7ed65), the manifest, the contract projection (current_pack + p08_closeout_p09_review_foundation_descriptor + cp267_requirements/cp267_no_write_attestation), the registry/service/validators wiring, README#cp00-267, and three dedicated CP00-267 tests plus validator-script assertions: 150 units = P08:100 + P09:50; deliverable counts (hermes_evidence 42, claude_review 14, implementation 73, test 9, security_audit 8, ui 4) and the nine micro-title counts match; no unit-ID gaps/dupes; chain is contiguous (CP00-266.next=CP00-267, CP00-267.next=CP00-268, next_subphase RP07.P09.M03.S11). No-write/no-real-data boundaries are both attested (no_write_attestation all-false writes/runtime; synthetic_only; no_real_data) and verified by the complete absence of IO/network/process/DB calls in source (only test/model.test.js reads JSON fixtures); descriptor rows carry descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, raw_payload_included:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true. Authority boundary is upheld: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob), claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation=true; Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, no_hermes_runtime_receipt=true), not product runtime. The review-hardening forbidden_review_evidence list matches all required blockers (auth failure, zero-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing fields, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims), and the readiness auth-preflight produced valid JSON at exit 0. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this checkpoint because they are produced from this review downstream (closeout-pack:validate is a post-review gated command per command-evidence notes); their absence is not a blocker. Two P3 informational notes only; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Recommendation: PASS. Final enterprise-trust closure remains gated on human approval, which this read-only review does not and cannot grant.

findings.title: P09 security_audit/test/ui deliverables are descriptor-only review templates, not executed audits/tests/UI
findings.details: The pack deliverable distribution reports security_audit:8, test:9, ui:4. Inspection of the RP07.P09 sections (registry.js required_section_rows l.2924-2927 and service.js SEARCH_CORE_CP267_ROW_EXTRAS l.4349-4369) shows these are descriptor-only review-question/template rows (e.g. 'Permission bypass questions', 'Missing test questions', 'UI leak questions') carrying descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, permission_bypass_detected:false, leak_detected:false. No actual security audit, test execution, or UI rendering occurs. This is by design for the P09 review-foundation phase and is consistent with the manifest no_write_attestation and the descriptor-only boundary.
findings.recommendation: No code change required. Ensure the human approver interprets these counts as descriptor classifications, not completed security audits/test runs/UI; real audit/test/UI execution is deferred to downstream runtime-opening packs (CP00-268+).
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; accurately represented as descriptor-only in manifest implementation_summary and no_write_attestation, and confirmed in source. No action.

findings.title: index.js listed as active implementation ref but unmodified (expected via wildcard re-export)
findings.details: changed-file-scope.json active_pack_implementation_refs includes packages/search/src/index.js, but git_status_rows do not list it as modified. index.js consists solely of `export * from "./registry.js"; export * from "./service.js"; export * from "./validators.js";`, so the new CP00-267 exports (createSearchCoreCp267* and validateSearchCoreCp267*) surface automatically without editing the barrel. This is correct behavior, not a missing change.
findings.recommendation: No action; behavior is correct. Optionally note in scope tooling that wildcard re-export barrels may appear in implementation refs without a corresponding diff.
findings.post_review_disposition: Verified correct; new CP00-267 symbols are re-exported automatically by the unchanged barrel. No action.
